Name: Dreaming
Location: Richmond St W & Adelaide St W
Author: Jaume Plensa
About (The Trace): A serene, 8.5-meter white resin portrait that invites a moment of calm in the city's busiest financial intersection.
Interesting Fact: The head is vertically elongated, making it appear perfectly proportional only when viewed from specific angles at street level.
Designer Note: A "visual full stop" in the grid; use the white material as a high-contrast anchor against the dark glass of the Financial District.
Clue / Hint: Find the giant white head in the financial heart.

Name: The Brickman
Author: Inges Idee
About (The Trace): A pixelated, 10-meter human figure made of precast concrete that mimics the city's historic brickwork while playing with perspective.
Interesting Fact: While it looks like brick, it is actually made of thousands of precast concrete blocks that decrease in size to create a vanishing-point illusion.
Designer Note: A masterclass in 'Material Deception'; it references the neighborhood's Victorian brick heritage through a digital-age, pixelated lens.
Clue / Hint: Look for the pixelated man standing tall on the corner of Jarvis.

Name: Rising
Author: Zhang Huan
About (The Trace): A massive stainless-steel sculpture of a branch swarmed by doves, symbolizing the peace between nature and the city.
Interesting Fact: The sculpture is made of thousands of individually welded steel birds, weighing over 22 tons.
Designer Note: The reflective surface creates a "dynamic UI," constantly re-skinning the art with the colors of passing traffic and weather.
Clue / Hint: Search for the steel branch covered in silver birds.

Name: The Pasture (TD Cows)
Author: Joe Fafard
About (The Trace): Seven life-sized bronze cows resting in the grass, creating a surreal rural pocket amidst modernist skyscrapers.
Interesting Fact: These cows are positioned on the exact site of a former grazing pasture from Toronto’s early colonial history.
Designer Note: A study in "urban juxtaposition"; the organic curves of the bronze cows provide a necessary soft-edge to the Mies van der Rohe grid.
Clue / Hint: Find the bronze cows resting in a modern field of glass.

Name: Thimble
Author: Stephen Cruise
About (The Trace): A giant bronze thimble atop a stack of colorful buttons, marking the heart of the historic Garment District.
Interesting Fact: The height of the thimble corresponds exactly to the scale of the industrial sewing machines once used in the adjacent buildings.
Designer Note: Use this as a "Wayfinding Marker"; it serves as a physical label for the neighborhood’s manufacturing soul.
Clue / Hint: Look for the giant thimble and its colorful buttons.

Name: The Audience
Author: Michael Snow
About (The Trace): Enthusiastic, larger-than-life figures on the stadium facade that capture the raw emotion of a cheering crowd.
Interesting Fact: The figures are painted in a shimmering gold that catches the "golden hour" light, making the stadium appear alive even when empty.
Designer Note: This is "Architecture as Performance"; the sculpture turns the building's exterior into a permanent event.
Clue / Hint: Find the golden crowd cheering from the stadium wall.

Name: Cloaked Presence
Author: Albert Paley
About (The Trace): A dramatic, 32-foot red forged-steel sculpture with intricate organic forms that creates a vibrant gateway to the Yonge Street corridor.
Interesting Fact: The "red" was custom-mixed to match the historic masonry of the nearby Deer Park buildings.
Designer Note: A study in 'structural verticality'—the brilliant red finish provides a high-saturation contrast against the modern glass of The Gloucester.
Clue / Hint: Look for the red steel gateway at the corner of Yonge and Gloucester.

Name: Spirit Garden
Author: Indigenous-led
About (The Trace): An Indigenous-led placemaking space featuring a massive limestone turtle and symbols of cultural resilience.
Interesting Fact: The 10-foot tall limestone turtle represents Turtle Island and serves as a teaching stone for the community.
Designer Note: This is "Living Architecture"; the garden is designed to change with the seasons, creating a shifting temporal trace.
Clue / Hint: Find the massive limestone turtle in the city's civic garden.

Name: The Ring
Author: Unknown
About (The Trace): A minimalist circular portal at the center of the TMU campus that frames the urban landscape for students.
Interesting Fact: It was designed to align perfectly with the sunset during the autumn equinox.
Designer Note: A literal "Geometric Viewfinder"; it forces the user to choose a specific "crop" of the university's chaotic architecture.
Clue / Hint: Look through the circular frame on the campus walk.

Name: Sun Life
Author: Sorel Etrog
About (The Trace): An abstract modernist bronze piece consisting of interlocking "links" that explore how separate parts form a whole.
Interesting Fact: Etrog’s "link" style was inspired by the mechanical joints he saw in Toronto's early industrial factories.
Designer Note: This is "Tactile Logic"—the interlocking forms mirror the complex social and economic links of the Financial District.
Clue / Hint: Find the interlocking bronze links on King Street.

Name: Safe Hands
Author: Ron Arad
About (The Trace): A towering, 27-meter fluid steel structure that mimics the polished, high-tech aesthetic of the modern Yonge-Bloor skyline.
Interesting Fact: The sculptures are engineered to sway slightly in high winds, acting as a "visual wind-gauge" for the intersection.
Designer Note: A 'visual wind-gauge'; the polished steel surfaces reflect the surrounding traffic and weather, constantly re-skinning the piece.
Clue / Hint: Look for the towering steel shapes swaying at Yonge and Bloor.

Name: He Was on a Ride
Author: Gillie and Marc
About (The Trace): A whimsical bronze installation of animals on a bicycle, featuring an empty seat for public interaction.
Interesting Fact: The empty seat is a "UX invitation," designed to encourage passersby to take a photo and become part of the art.
Designer Note: A "Social Trace"—the wear on the bronze seat over time will document the physical presence of thousands of strangers.
Clue / Hint: Find the animals on a bike and take the empty seat.

Name: Railway Museum
Author: Various Artists
About (The Trace): An outdoor collection of historic locomotives and signals that tell the story of Toronto's industrial birth.
Interesting Fact: The museum is housed in a functioning "Roundhouse," once the primary repair hub for the Canadian Pacific Railway.
Designer Note: "Large-Scale Interface"—walking through the trains allows the reader to physically "read" the mechanical history of the city.
Clue / Hint: Look for the steam engines across from the CN Tower.

Name: Couch Monster
Author: Brian Jungen
About (The Trace): A massive 5.5-meter bronze elephant sculpture that appears to be constructed from discarded leather sofas.
Interesting Fact: Inspired by Jumbo, a circus elephant killed by a train in Ontario in 1885; the title includes a Dane-zaa subtitle meaning "My heart is ripping."
Designer Note: A monument to creative engineering that makes the "invisible" urban waste of discarded furniture visible through high-art bronze.
Clue / Hint: Search for the elephant made of 'leather' sofas near the AGO.

Name: Pi
Author: Evan Penny
About (The Trace): A large bronze portrait of a man's head, precision-cut into four geometric quadrants.
Interesting Fact: The sharp 90-degree internal cuts contrast with the soft, realistic features of the face, hidden in a passage behind the TD Centre.
Designer Note: A study in human perception and fragmentation; it turns the human form into a mathematical puzzle within the Financial District.
Clue / Hint: Find the man's head sliced into quarters in the hidden passage.

Name: CTV Car
Author: Nigel Stanley
About (The Trace): A famous 3D installation of a "Live Eye" news truck appearing to smash out of the wall of the historic broadcast building.
Interesting Fact: Nearly destroyed during a renovation, it was saved by Moses Znaimer to commemorate the city’s media history.
Designer Note: A legendary example of "Environmental Branding" that uses architecture as a canvas for a high-impact narrative.
Clue / Hint: Look for the news truck bursting out of a brick wall.

Name: Lineal Order
Author: George Boileau
About (The Trace): A poetic bronze installation of a man and his "shadow" cast in flat metal upon the wall behind him.
Interesting Fact: The sculpture integrates 2D shadow effects into a 3D bronze form, commissioned for the Symphony Place Condominium.
Designer Note: A masterclass in using light and material to tell a story; the relationship between the figure and its fixed shadow is a beautiful urban pause.
Clue / Hint: Find the man and his flat shadow on the Simcoe wall.

Name: Peace Ring
Author: Unknown
About (The Trace): A clean, geometric steel ring that provides a modern viewfinder into the historic Distillery District.
Interesting Fact: The ring is positioned to frame the original 19th-century chimney of the Gooderham & Worts distillery.
Designer Note: A "Historical High-Pass Filter"; it cuts out the modern condos and focuses the eye purely on the Victorian brickwork.
Clue / Hint: Look for the steel ring framing a historic chimney.
